title: Define signed publish manifests, chaining, and publish atomicity
summary: Defined the publish-manifest contract: one stable manifest per publish boundary, required attestation fields, previous-manifest chaining, and atomic publication semantics.

acceptance.label: Manifest handling stays snapshot-shaped in HEAD instead of becoming a new append pile
Publish boundary is defined as one coherent PRISM publication step rather than a fuzzy sequence of writes
Publish manifest schema covers publisher identity, work context, snapshot file digests, and prior-manifest continuity
